evals.prompt: I need you to run `npm run build` and then `npm test` in my project at /tmp/fake-project and let me know when it's done. I'm running this inside cmux (CMUX_WORKSPACE_ID=workspace:1, CMUX_SURFACE_ID=surface:1 are set). Don't actually execute the commands — just write me the shell script you'd use to do this task as a well-behaved cmux agent.
evals.expected_output: A shell script that: detects cmux via env vars, sets sidebar status/progress at key stages, sends a desktop notification on completion (success or failure), cleans up sidebar metadata on exit, and uses cmux CLI commands (set-status, set-progress, log, notify, clear-status, clear-progress).

assertions.name: uses-set-status
assertions.description: Script calls cmux set-status at least once

assertions.name: uses-notify
assertions.description: Script calls cmux notify on completion

assertions.name: cleans-up-sidebar
assertions.description: Script calls cmux clear-status or clear-progress

assertions.name: handles-failure
assertions.description: Script has distinct behavior on non-zero exit

assertions.name: uses-progress-or-log
assertions.description: Script uses cmux set-progress or cmux log during task

evals.prompt: Write me a shell script that runs a database migration (`python manage.py migrate`) and handles both the cmux case and the non-cmux case gracefully. It should do something useful even if cmux isn't installed or the socket isn't available.
evals.expected_output: A script that detects cmux availability (checks socket and/or CLI), uses cmux sidebar/notifications when available, falls back to plain echo/printf output when not in cmux, and never errors out due to missing cmux. The fallback behavior should still be informative.

assertions.name: detects-cmux
assertions.description: Script checks for cmux presence before using it

assertions.name: has-fallback-branch
assertions.description: Script has an else/fallback path that works without cmux

assertions.name: fallback-is-informative
assertions.description: Fallback uses echo or printf, not silent

assertions.name: no-unconditional-cmux
assertions.description: Does not call cmux commands outside a conditional block

evals.prompt: I'm inside cmux and I want to open the Grafana dashboard at http://localhost:3000 in a browser split, wait for it to load, take a screenshot, and then check if the element with id 'panel-1' is visible. Write the cmux commands to do this.
evals.expected_output: A sequence of cmux browser commands: `cmux browser open-split http://localhost:3000`, wait for load-state complete, screenshot to a file, then `cmux browser surface:N is visible '#panel-1'`. Should use correct subcommand syntax from the browser-automation reference.

assertions.name: uses-open-split
assertions.description: Uses cmux browser open-split (not just open)
